title: Novena to the Sacred Heart of Jesus
description: A traditional nine-day novena to the Sacred Heart of Jesus, prayed in preparation for the Solemnity of the Sacred Heart (Friday after Corpus Christi) or at any time.

days.title: Day One — The Love of the Sacred Heart
days.body:
O Lord Jesus Christ, Thou hast said, 'Learn from me, for I am meek and humble of heart.' On this first day of our novena, we contemplate Thy Sacred Heart as the furnace of divine love, burning ceaselessly for each soul.

Thy Heart was pierced for our offenses. Every beat of that Heart was an act of love — for us, for sinners, for those who would never know Thee. How great is this love that descends to seek the lost!

O Sacred Heart of Jesus, I consecrate to Thee this day my thoughts, desires, and all the affections of my heart. Let me love what Thou lovest and avoid what displeases Thee. Grant me a share of Thy meekness and humility.

Sacred Heart of Jesus, I place my trust in Thee.

Our Father... Hail Mary... Glory Be...

days.title: Day Two — Reparation
days.body:
O Heart of Jesus, burning with love for us and so little loved in return! How often have we offended Thee by sin, by coldness, by indifference! Today we come in a spirit of reparation.

The Church has always taught that the Heart of Jesus is grieved by the ingratitude of souls. Yet this same Heart opens wide in mercy to those who return to it with sorrow.

O Lord, I am sorry for every sin by which I have wounded Thy Heart. Accept this day as an act of reparation. May every prayer I offer, every sacrifice I make, be a small comfort to Thy Sacred Heart.

Sacred Heart of Jesus, have mercy on us.

Our Father... Hail Mary... Glory Be...

days.title: Day Three — Trust in Providence
days.body:
O Jesus, meek and gentle, Thou hast told us not to be anxious for the morrow, for our heavenly Father knows what we need. Yet how easily we grow anxious, worrying over things beyond our control.

Thy Sacred Heart is the source of all Providence. Nothing escapes Thy love or knowledge. Even the hairs of our head are numbered. How much more does Thy Heart attend to our deepest needs!

On this third day, I lay before Thy Sacred Heart all my anxieties, fears, and uncertainties. Take them into Thy Heart. Replace them with Thy peace. Let me trust not in my own plans but in Thy wise and loving Providence.

Sacred Heart of Jesus, I place my trust in Thee.

Our Father... Hail Mary... Glory Be...

days.title: Day Four — The Heart That Suffers with Us
days.body:
O Lord Jesus, in the Garden of Gethsemane Thy Sacred Heart was sorrowful unto death. Thou art no stranger to anguish, loneliness, or the feeling of abandonment. Thou hast borne every sorrow that we bear.

'Come to me, all you who labor and are heavy laden, and I will give you rest.' This is the invitation of Thy Heart to every suffering soul.

On this fourth day I bring before Thy Heart all those who suffer — the sick, the grieving, the lonely, the despairing. And I bring my own suffering too. Let me find rest in Thy Heart. Let Thy consolation reach those in the deepest darkness.

Sacred Heart of Jesus, comfort all who suffer.

Our Father... Hail Mary... Glory Be...

days.title: Day Five — The Heart That Forgives
days.body:
O Heart of Jesus, from the Cross Thou didst cry out: 'Father, forgive them, for they know not what they do.' Thy forgiveness has no limit. The good thief, with barely a breath left, received from Thee the promise of Paradise.

How often have I despaired of forgiveness, believing my sins too great, my failures too many? Yet no sin is beyond the reach of Thy mercy, so long as the soul turns to Thee in contrition.

On this fifth day, I ask Thee for the grace of a deep and lasting contrition. Forgive all my sins — known and unknown, mortal and venial, forgotten and remembered. And give me the grace to forgive those who have sinned against me.

Sacred Heart of Jesus, forgive us and make us one.

Our Father... Hail Mary... Glory Be...

days.title: Day Six — The Eucharistic Heart
days.body:
O Lord Jesus, at the Last Supper Thou didst give us the most extravagant gift of Thy love: Thyself, truly present under the appearance of bread and wine. The Eucharist is the ongoing gift of Thy Sacred Heart to the world.

Every Mass is Calvary made present. Every Holy Communion is a meeting of heart to heart — Thy Heart to mine. What love is this, that gives itself so completely, so humbly, so repeatedly!

On this sixth day I pray for a deeper love for the Eucharist. Let me approach Holy Communion with greater reverence, preparation, and gratitude. May the Eucharist truly transform me into Thy likeness.

Sacred Heart of Jesus, truly present in the Blessed Sacrament, I adore Thee.

Our Father... Hail Mary... Glory Be...

days.title: Day Seven — The Heart and Our Lady
days.body:
O Lord Jesus, Thy Sacred Heart was formed beneath the Immaculate Heart of Mary. From the first moment of Thy life on earth, the Hearts of Jesus and Mary have been united. She who pondered all things in her heart now intercedes for us from Heaven.

To come to the Heart of Jesus through the heart of Mary is the way of the saints. She leads us always toward her Son.

On this seventh day I consecrate myself anew to the Immaculate Heart of Mary, asking her to place me ever closer to Thy Sacred Heart. May her intercession obtain for me all that I need to grow in holiness.

Sacred Heart of Jesus and Immaculate Heart of Mary, be our refuge.

Our Father... Hail Mary... Glory Be...

days.title: Day Eight — The Twelve Promises
days.body:
O Lord Jesus, Thou didst appear to St. Margaret Mary Alacoque and reveal to her the riches of Thy Sacred Heart, promising twelve special graces to those devoted to it — including peace in families, consolation in troubles, and the grace of final perseverance.

These promises flow from the inexhaustible love of Thy Heart. They are not magic formulas but the fruit of a living relationship of love and trust between the soul and Thee.

On this eighth day I renew my devotion to Thy Sacred Heart. Let this novena not end with these nine days but become a daily way of life — returning to Thy Heart in prayer, in the sacraments, and in acts of love.

Sacred Heart of Jesus, let my heart be one with Thine.

Our Father... Hail Mary... Glory Be...

days.title: Day Nine — Consecration to the Sacred Heart
days.body:
O most Holy Heart of Jesus, fountain of every blessing, I adore Thee, I love Thee, and with lively sorrow for my sins I offer Thee this poor heart of mine. Make me humble, patient, pure, and wholly obedient to Thy will.

Grant, good Jesus, that I may live in Thee and for Thee. Protect me in my dangers. Console me in my afflictions. Give me health of body, assistance in my temporal needs, Thy blessing on all that I do, and the grace of a holy death.

I ask these graces for myself and for all those I have prayed for during these nine days. Grant them according to Thy wisdom and mercy.

Within Thy Heart I place all my intentions. Sacred Heart of Jesus, Thy Kingdom come.

Our Father... Hail Mary... Glory Be... Amen.

title: Novena to St. Joseph
description: A nine-day novena to St. Joseph, patron of the universal Church, workers, fathers, and a holy death. Traditionally prayed before his feast day (March 19) or at any time of need.

days.title: Day One — Joseph, Husband of Mary
days.body:
O Blessed Joseph, God chose you above all men to be the husband of the Most Pure Virgin Mary, Mother of God. What incomparable grace! You were called to stand beside the one whom all generations would call blessed, to love and protect her with all the chastity and devotion of your heart.

You show us that true love is not selfish possession but selfless service. You loved Mary not for what she could give you, but in imitation of God's own pure love for her.

O St. Joseph, obtain for me and for all those I love the grace of pure and selfless love — in marriage, in family life, and in all our relationships. May we love as you loved.

Hail Mary... Our Father... Glory Be...

days.title: Day Two — Joseph, Foster Father of the Son of God
days.body:
O holy Joseph, what incomprehensible dignity! The eternal Son of God, through whom all things were made, called you 'father,' held your hand, learned from your lips, and obeyed you as a child obeys a parent.

You who held the Word Incarnate, who taught the Lord of the Sabbath the carpenter's trade, who protected Him from Herod's sword — what trust the Father placed in you!

O St. Joseph, intercede for all fathers, that they may embrace their vocation with the same faithfulness and tenderness you showed. Pray for families in crisis, for children without fathers, and for all who struggle to raise their children in the faith.

Hail Mary... Our Father... Glory Be...

days.title: Day Three — Joseph, Man of Silence
days.body:
O silent Joseph! In all the sacred pages of the Gospel, not a single word of yours is recorded. Yet your life speaks volumes. In silence you heard the angel's message, in silence you arose in the night to flee to Egypt, in silence you carried the world's burdens.

We live in an age of noise, restlessness, and endless distraction. We have lost the gift of interior silence where God speaks to the soul. Your example calls us back.

O St. Joseph, teach me the value of silence — silence of tongue, silence of mind, silence of heart. In that silence may I hear the voice of God and respond with the same prompt obedience that marked your holy life.

Hail Mary... Our Father... Glory Be...

days.title: Day Four — Joseph, Patron of Workers
days.body:
O Blessed Joseph, you were a craftsman — a carpenter who worked with your hands, who knew the smell of sawdust and the ache of labor. Pope Pius XII proclaimed you patron of workers, and rightly so, for you sanctified ordinary work by your faithful diligence.

All honest labor has dignity because it participates in God's creative work and provides for the needs of others. But how easy it is to reduce work to mere survival, to lose sight of its sacred character!

O St. Joseph, patron of workers, pray for all who labor — especially those whose work is dangerous, underpaid, or unrecognized. Give them dignity, safety, and just wages. And help me to offer my own daily work to God as you offered yours.

Hail Mary... Our Father... Glory Be...

days.title: Day Five — Joseph, Protector of the Church
days.body:
O Blessed Joseph, just as God entrusted to your care the Holy Family of Nazareth — Jesus and Mary — so has He entrusted to your patronage the Church, the family of God on earth. You are Guardian of the Universal Church.

The Church today faces trials from without and within. She has need of your fatherly protection, your prudence, and your intercession before the throne of God.

O St. Joseph, protect Holy Mother Church. Guard the Pope and all bishops and priests. Pray for the persecuted, for the lukewarm to be enkindled, and for those who have left the faith to return. May the Church ever grow in holiness until the Lord's return.

Hail Mary... Our Father... Glory Be...

days.title: Day Six — Joseph, Man of Faith
days.body:
O holy Joseph, your faith was tested in ways few of us can imagine. An inexplicable pregnancy, a midnight flight, years of exile in a foreign land, the mystery of a Son who was and was not yours. Yet you trusted, always trusted.

When you could not understand, you believed. When you could not see the plan, you obeyed. This is the highest form of faith: trust in God's goodness even in the dark.

O St. Joseph, I bring before you today the uncertainties and confusions of my own life. Strengthen my faith where it is weak. Pray that I may trust God's plan even when I cannot see it, and that I may take one obedient step at a time, as you did.

Hail Mary... Our Father... Glory Be...

days.title: Day Seven — Joseph, Our Intercessor
days.body:
O Blessed Joseph, the saints and Doctors of the Church — among them St. Teresa of Ávila — have praised your power of intercession and urged the faithful to have great confidence in your prayers.

If Jesus, who loves His Mother above all creatures, grants her every request, how generously must He respond to the requests of you, His foster father, who cared for Him with such tender faithfulness during His years of earthly childhood!

O St. Joseph, I now bring before you the intentions I have carried throughout this novena. Present them to our Lord Jesus. Obtain from Him whatever is for my true good and His greater glory. I trust in your intercession.

Hail Mary... Our Father... Glory Be...

days.title: Day Eight — Joseph, Patron of a Happy Death
days.body:
O most fortunate Joseph! Of all the deaths ever died, yours is called the happiest — for you died in the arms of Jesus and Mary, the most consoling presences the world has ever known. For this reason you are invoked as patron of the dying.

Death is the great certainty we prefer not to consider. Yet a good death — prepared for, met with faith and sacraments, with loved ones near — is a grace to pray for daily.

O St. Joseph, pray for me at the hour of my death. May I receive the last sacraments. May I die in a state of grace, with genuine contrition and trust in God's mercy. And may Jesus and Mary be near me as they were near you.

Hail Mary... Our Father... Glory Be...

days.title: Day Nine — Final Petition
days.body:
O glorious St. Joseph, we have spent these nine days meditating on your virtues and asking your intercession. You are the terror of demons, the consolation of the afflicted, and the powerful patron of all who call upon you with faith.

I now place before you the special intention for which I have prayed this novena. You who know our needs even before we express them, present this petition to Jesus your Son. Whether or not it is granted in the way I hope, let God's holy will be done. Give me the grace to accept His will as you accepted it — with trust, with silence, and with love.

St. Joseph, terror of demons and patron of a holy death, pray for us now and at the hour of our death. Amen.

Hail Mary... Our Father... Glory Be... Amen.
